name:
🔴𝗜𝗻𝘁𝗲́𝗿𝗲𝘀𝘀𝗮𝗻𝘁 : 𝗟𝗲 𝗠𝗶𝗻𝗶𝘀𝘁𝗿𝗲 𝗕𝘂𝗿𝘂𝗻𝗱𝗮𝗶𝘀 𝗱𝗲𝘀 @MAEBurundi, @Edbiziman 𝗲𝘀𝘁 𝗱𝗲́𝘀𝗶𝗴𝗻𝗲́ «  𝗖𝗼𝗽𝗿𝗲́𝘀𝗶𝗱𝗲𝗻𝘁 », 𝗱𝗲 𝗹𝗮 𝗿𝗲́𝘂𝗻𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝗺𝗶𝗻𝗶𝘀𝘁𝗲́𝗿𝗶𝗲𝗹𝗹𝗲 𝗽𝗿𝗲́𝗽𝗮𝗿𝗮𝘁𝗼𝗶𝗿𝗲 𝗱𝗲 𝗹𝗮 𝗧𝗜𝗖𝗔𝗗 𝟵.🇧🇮🤝🇯🇵
============================
Dans ...
description:
🔴𝗜𝗻𝘁𝗲́𝗿𝗲𝘀𝘀𝗮𝗻𝘁 : 𝗟𝗲 𝗠𝗶𝗻𝗶𝘀𝘁𝗿𝗲 𝗕𝘂𝗿𝘂𝗻𝗱𝗮𝗶𝘀 𝗱𝗲𝘀 @MAEBurundi, @Edbiziman 𝗲𝘀𝘁 𝗱𝗲́𝘀𝗶𝗴𝗻𝗲́ «  𝗖𝗼𝗽𝗿𝗲́𝘀𝗶𝗱𝗲𝗻𝘁 », 𝗱𝗲 𝗹𝗮 𝗿𝗲́𝘂𝗻𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝗺𝗶𝗻𝗶𝘀𝘁𝗲́𝗿𝗶𝗲𝗹𝗹𝗲 𝗽𝗿𝗲́𝗽𝗮𝗿𝗮𝘁𝗼𝗶𝗿𝗲 𝗱𝗲 𝗹𝗮 𝗧𝗜𝗖𝗔𝗗 𝟵.🇧🇮🤝🇯🇵
============================
Dans cette réunion tenue ce mardi 19 août 2025, le Ministre BIZIMANA, coprésident de la TICAD9, a exprimé sa gratitude au gouvernement japonais et à la ville de Yokohama pour leurs efforts dans la préparation de la conférence. 

🔹Il a également déclaré que : « 𝗟𝗲 𝘁𝗵𝗲̀𝗺𝗲  "𝗖𝗼-𝗰𝗿𝗲́𝗲𝗿 𝗱𝗲𝘀 𝘀𝗼𝗹𝘂𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻𝘀 𝗶𝗻𝗻𝗼𝘃𝗮𝗻𝘁𝗲𝘀" 𝗿𝗲𝗻𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗰𝗲𝗿𝗮𝗶𝘁 𝗹'𝗔𝗴𝗲𝗻𝗱𝗮 𝟮𝟬𝟲𝟯 𝗲𝘁 𝗹'𝗔𝗴𝗲𝗻𝗱𝗮 𝟮𝟬𝟯𝟬 𝗽𝗼𝘂𝗿 𝗹𝗲 𝗱𝗲́𝘃𝗲𝗹𝗼𝗽𝗽𝗲𝗺𝗲𝗻𝘁 𝗱𝘂𝗿𝗮𝗯𝗹𝗲 » et a exprimé son souhait de : « 𝗿𝗲𝗻𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗰𝗲𝗿 𝗱𝗮𝘃𝗮𝗻𝘁𝗮𝗴𝗲 𝗹𝗲 𝗽𝗮𝗿𝘁𝗲𝗻𝗮𝗿𝗶𝗮𝘁 𝗲𝗻𝘁𝗿𝗲 𝗹𝗲 𝗝𝗮𝗽𝗼𝗻 𝗲𝘁 𝗹𝗲𝘀 𝗽𝗮𝘆𝘀 𝗮𝗳𝗿𝗶𝗰𝗮𝗶𝗻𝘀 𝗴𝗿𝗮̂𝗰𝗲 𝗮̀ 𝗹𝗮 𝗧𝗜𝗖𝗔𝗗 𝟵 » .

🔹Le Ministre BIZIMANA a coprésidé cette réunion aux côtés du Ministre Japonais des Affaires étrangères (@MofaJapan_en ) , IWAYA Takeshi.

🔹Cette reunion a vu la présence de l'Amb. @DCP_Haddadi, vice-présidente de la Commission de l' @_AfricanUnion, Mme Cristina Duarte (@Duarte_UNOSAA ), secrétaire générale adjointe des @UN, Mme @ahunnaeziakonwa, Secrétaire général adjoint et Directrice du @UNDPAfrica, M. Hiroshi Matano, vice-président exécutif de la @MIGA, et un membre de l'équipe de direction du Groupe de la @Banquemondiale.

🔹Les participants ont unanimement partagé la volonté de coopérer pour assurer le succès de la TICAD 9. Ensemble, ils vont trouver de nouvelles solutions aux défis auxquels sont confrontées l'Afrique et la communauté internationale, en s'appuyant sur les atouts du Japon et de l'Afrique.

#lesbeautesdecheznous #akezanet #diplomatie #Burundi @UNOSAA1 @WorldBank

name:
Appointment of an African Union Special Envoy for Gabon and Equatorial Guinea.

The Chairperson of the African Union Commission, H.E.
description:
Appointment of an African Union Special Envoy for Gabon and Equatorial Guinea.

The Chairperson of the African Union Commission, H.E. Mahmoud Ali  Youssouf, has appointed H.E. Albert Shingiro, former Minister of Foreign  Affairs of the Republic of Burundi, as Special Envoy to support the  Republic of Gabon and the Republic of Equatorial Guinea in the  implementation of the judgment rendered by the International Court of  Justice on 19 May 2025, concerning the land and maritime delimitation  and sovereignty over the islands of Mbanié, Conga and Cocoteros.

This appointment follows the quadripartite meeting held on 14  February 2026 under the auspices of the African Union, during which both  parties reaffirmed their commitment to a coordinated and peaceful  implementation of the Court’s decision.

Through this designation, the African Union reaffirms its commitment  to the peaceful settlement of disputes and to the promotion of African  solutions grounded in international law.

In the discharge of his mandate, the Special Envoy will undertake the  necessary consultations with both States in order to facilitate  consensus on the modalities for implementing the judgment, in accordance  with the principles enshrined in the Constitutive Act of the African  Union.

The Chairperson of the Commission @ymahmoudali expressed his full confidence in  the experience and expertise of H.E. Albert Shingiro to contribute to  the effective implementation of the judgment and to the strengthening of  stability and good neighbourly relations between the two countries.

English: https://au.int/en/pressreleases/20260325/appointment-african-union-special-envoy-gabon-and-equatorial-guinea

name: Following the African Union’s permanent membership in the G20 and South Africa’s historic 2025 Presidency, the next priority is strategic preparation for the 2026 United States G20 Presidency.
description:
Following the African Union’s permanent membership in the G20 and South Africa’s historic 2025 Presidency, the next priority is strategic preparation for the 2026 United States G20 Presidency. #G20Miami

The AU will convene a Strategic AU G20 Retreat on 27–28 April 2026 in Malabo, Equatorial Guinea, hosted by the Republic of Equatorial Guinea. 

The Retreat aims to discuss:-
1.Lessons from South Africa’s Presidency
2. 2026 U.S. G20 priorities & opportunities for Africa
3. Common AU position under #Agenda2063
4.Stronger coordination & leadership transition 5. Roadmap for Africa’s G20 engagement

Stay tuned for updates.

title: Speech by H.E.
description:
Speech by H.E. the Ambassador of the Republic of #Burundi, @willynyamitwe on the Occasion of Nile Day, February 22, 2025, #AddisAbaba, #Ethiopia

Your Excellency Mr President of the Federal Democratic Republic of Ethiopia;
Excellencies, Ladies and Gentlemen, all protocol observed,

It is with great honor and immense joy that I stand before you today on this special occasion dedicated to our shared river, the Nile. Today, we celebrate the 19th edition of Nile Day, an event that brings us together in a spirit of fraternity, cooperation, and reflection on the future of this invaluable resource.

First and foremost, on behalf of His Excellency the Minister of Environment, Agriculture, and Livestock of Burundi, and on my own behalf, I wish to convey the warmest greetings of His Excellency Mr. Évariste Ndayishimiye, President of the Republic of Burundi.

I would also like to express my deep gratitude to the Government and people of Ethiopia for their warm welcome and legendary hospitality. Ethiopia is a key country in the Nile Basin, a committed actor in the management of this vital resource, and its leadership in regional cooperation deserves recognition.

Nile Day is more than just a commemoration. It is a symbol of our collective commitment to the responsible and equitable management of this shared resource. It reminds us that the Nile knows no borders; it connects our nations, irrigates our lands, nourishes our populations, and powers our industries.

This river, stretching over 6,800 kilometers, is a lifeline for nearly 500 million people across Africa. But beyond its hydrological and economic importance, the Nile is a bridge between our peoples, an opportunity for cooperation and shared prosperity.

Since the establishment of the Nile Basin Initiative, our states have demonstrated that working together for harmonious and inclusive development is possible. Dialogue and collective action have facilitated the implementation of tangible projects, improving the lives of our citizens.

Burundi is proud to be an active member of this initiative, which has fostered continuous consultation between riparian states and provided solutions to common challenges. Among the most significant achievements, I would like to highlight the Rusumo hydropower project, which has added 27 MW to our national power grid, thereby improving energy access for our people and supporting economic growth.

If we are gathered here today, it is also to recognize the challenges that remain and to discuss the best strategies to overcome them together.

Our region faces several major challenges such as rapid population growth, the effects of climate change, soil erosion and deforestation, disparities in water access and utilization.

These challenges can only be addressed through stronger cooperation and an integrated approach to water resource management.

A Burundian proverb says, “Ubirigiri bugira babiri,” which translates to “Unity is strength.” We must draw from this wisdom to make the Nile a driver of sustainable development for our nations.

It is in this spirit that Burundi ratified the CFA in 2023, reaffirming our deep commitment to regional cooperation and our aspiration to build a future where the Nile serves as a true engine of development and shared prosperity.

As we prepare to transition from the Nile Basin Initiative to the Nile Basin Commission, we must ensure that this transformation is built on solid foundations, rooted in mutual trust, equity, and shared responsibility.

New challenges will undoubtedly arise, but we must remain united in our common vision “To promote sustainable socio-economic development through the equitable use of Nile Basin waters and the fair distribution of benefits from this shared resource.”

Together, we can transform the Nile into a symbol of shared prosperity and lasting peace.

May this day mark the beginning of a renewed momentum for our organization and for the well-being of our people.

I thank you.

#NileDay2025

title: La réunion de ce samedi 12 août a été reportée car l’Ambassadeur du  qui assure la Présidence du Conseil de Paix et de Sécurité de l’Union Africaine (CPS) au mois d’août 2023 Amb.
description:
La réunion de ce samedi 12 août a été reportée car l’Ambassadeur du #Burundi qui assure la Présidence du Conseil de Paix et de Sécurité de l’Union Africaine (CPS) au mois d’août 2023 Amb. @willynyamitwe était au Nigéria pour le Sommet de la CEDEAO. 

La réunion aura lieu lundi 14 août. 

Il n’y a pas de division parmi les membres du CPS qui exercent leur mandat conformément au Protocole portant création du Conseil de Paix et de Sécurité ainsi qu’à l’Acte Constitutif de l’Union Africaine.

title: Invited by the United Kingdom’s Ambassador to  H.E.

Président @GeneralNeva à  16 Juillet 2023: “Depuis juillet 2022, l’honneur m’a été fait d’assurer la Présidence  en exercice de la Communauté de l'Afrique de l'Est (CAE), dont le dynamisme et l’imp...
description:
Président @GeneralNeva à #Nairobi, 16 Juillet 2023: “Depuis juillet 2022, l’honneur m’a été fait d’assurer la Présidence  en exercice de la Communauté de l'Afrique de l'Est (CAE), dont le dynamisme et l’impact n’ont cessé de croître bien que nos économies n’échappent pas aux chocs mondiaux résultant des effets persistants de Covid-19; le conflit russo-ukrainien, les conditions financières mondiales difficiles et les conséquences du changement climatique.
 
En effet, depuis sa création en 1999 et conformément à sa vision d’être une Afrique de l’Est prospère, compétitive, sûre, stable et politiquement unie, la Communauté de l’Afrique de l’Est (@jumuiya) a créé une union douanière en 2005, un marché commun en 2010 et le protocole sur l'union monétaire a été signé en novembre 2013.”

title: Extract from my statement in my capacity as Chair of the @_AfricanUnion Permanent Representatives’ Committee (PRC), delivered during the High-Level Opening Segment of the African Union Retreat on  ...
description:
Extract from my statement in my capacity as Chair of the @_AfricanUnion Permanent Representatives’ Committee (PRC), delivered during the High-Level Opening Segment of the African Union Retreat on #G20 Engagements for 2026, in #Malabo:

“The success of our permanent membership in the G20 will not be measured by our presence at the table, but by the tangible impact of our contributions. It will be measured by our ability to deliver results that accelerate Africa’s industrialization, address the heavy debt burdens facing our economies, and promote inclusive and equitable growth for the 1.4 billion Africans we represent here—and ultimately to realize the aspirations of Agenda 2063: The Africa We Want.

“Let us use this retreat to consolidate our unity, refine our strategies, and prepare to engage the 2026 G20 Presidency with clarity, coherence, and unwavering determination.

“But beyond the technical aspects, recent developments call for our serious attention. When the voice of South Africa, the icon and standard-bearer of the continent within the G20, is set aside by the country holding the rotating presidency in 2026, a deeper question arises for us as the African Union.

What should be the concrete expression of our solidarity? Can it be limited to statements of principle, or must it be embodied in clear, deliberate, and collective positions? And above all, can we truly consider that sidelining one of us does not implicate the entire continent?

For if the voice of one is weakened, the voice of all is diminished. And if the representation of one can be challenged without a collective response, then it is our very cohesion that is being tested.

This moment must therefore prompt a clear-eyed, clear-headed and demanding reflection: are we ready to make our unity a genuine lever of influence, even when it requires speaking with one voice, firmly and consistently, in the face of imbalances in the international system?

May this retreat enable us not only to prepare, but also to take a decisive step forward, towards an African solidarity that is fully assumed, strategic, and action-oriented.

title: Embassy to Ethiopia welcome the AU–G20 Retreat in Malabo and commend Equatorial Guinea for hosting.
description: #Burundi Embassy to Ethiopia welcome the AU–G20 Retreat in Malabo and commend Equatorial Guinea for hosting. Building on South Africa’s 2025 Presidency, the #AU moves forward with a united voice toward the United States 2026 Presidency under Agenda 2063. #G20Miami

title: Following the African Union’s permanent membership in the G20 and South Africa’s historic 2025 Presidency, the next priority is strategic preparation for the 2026 United States G20 Presidency.
description:
Following the African Union’s permanent membership in the G20 and South Africa’s historic 2025 Presidency, the next priority is strategic preparation for the 2026 United States G20 Presidency. #G20Miami

The AU will convene a Strategic AU G20 Retreat on 27–28 April 2026 in Malabo, Equatorial Guinea, hosted by the Republic of Equatorial Guinea. 

The Retreat aims to discuss:-
1.Lessons from South Africa’s Presidency
2. 2026 U.S. G20 priorities & opportunities for Africa
3. Common AU position under #Agenda2063
4.Stronger coordination & leadership transition 5. Roadmap for Africa’s G20 engagement

Stay tuned for updates.
